headline: FIRST-BASELINE assessment for Open Bionics (rotation queue, never previously assessed; published rank 1, composite 97.5, Exemplary band). Research strongly confirms Exemplary baseline: (1) 2025 launch of Hero Pro and Hero RGD (wireless, waterproof, function-when-detached) extends accessibility; (2) Hero Arm now available for children as young as 5 (lowered from 8) — pediatric multi-grip myoelectric leadership; (3) ~801 US locations; (4) 5x cost reduction vs. traditional bionic prosthetics addresses <1% affordability gap; (5) Mission-aligned business model (turning disabilities into 'superpowers'); (6) Iron Man and Frozen-themed magnetic covers co-designed with children. Modest evidence gaps: limited public data on long-term outcomes, internal employee culture, and supply-chain ethics. Net assessment: composite 95.0 vs published 97.5 — sub-threshold delta of -2.5 pts; band remains Exemplary. Confirmation, no proposal applied.
key_evidence: 2025: Hero Pro and Hero RGD launched — wireless, waterproof, function-when-detached, advancing technical accessibility (Open Bionics product page).
Hero Arm now available for children as young as 5 (lowered from 8) — pediatric multi-grip myoelectric leadership.
Available in 800+ US locations through clinical partner network including Hanger Clinic.
Cost positioned at ~5x less than traditional bionic prosthetics ($100K) addressing <1% affordability gap.
Co-designed with children: themed magnetic covers (Marvel, Disney, Star Wars) — community-input design pattern.
Mission-aligned brand: 'Disabilities turned to superpowers' (PTC Third Angle podcast).
Evidence gaps: limited public outcome data on long-term use rates, employee culture/wellbeing, supply-chain ethics, and per-population access disparities.

evidence.finding: Hero Arm — multi-grip myoelectric prosthetic; Hero Pro and Hero RGD launched 2025; works wireless and waterproof.
Hero Arm available for children as young as 5; pediatric design features include ventilation and changeable magnetic covers.
Open Bionics' heroic robotics mission: making prosthetics accessible to people who otherwise could not afford them.
Reviewed as the most advanced prosthetic arm 2026; confirms continued market leadership.
Hero Arm distributed through 800+ Hanger Clinic locations in US; clinical partner network active.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 4.4 (-0.1). Strong product-feedback awareness (community-input designs); minor gap in publicly disclosed structured suffering-detection mechanism for non-users (people who try and discontinue).
scoring_rationale.EMP: 4.6 (+0.1). Children co-designing magnetic covers, 'superpowers' framing — E2 Perspective-Taking and E5 Cultural Empathy strongly evidenced (community confirms genuine).
scoring_rationale.ACT: 4.5 (unchanged). 2025 Hero Pro/RGD launch and pediatric-5 lowering are documented capability expansions; AC2 Proportionality and AC3 Efficacy strong.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 4.5 (unchanged). EQ4 Access Design strongly evidenced through 5x cost reduction and pediatric-5 availability; EQ2 Priority for Vulnerable structurally embedded.
scoring_rationale.BND: 4.4 (-0.1). B1 Self-Sustainability has limited public evidence — small UK company scaling globally; minor risk consideration.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 4.2 (-0.3). AB3 Transparency: less public reporting of independent evaluation, longitudinal outcome tracking, or failure disclosures than would warrant 4.5; published score may overstate Tier-5 evidence.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 4.5 (unchanged). S1 Root Cause Orientation: addresses prosthetic-affordability root cause; S5 Coalitional Compassion: clinical partner network model.
scoring_rationale.INT: 4.5 (unchanged). I2 Non-Performance: mission-aligned business model with documented children-first design choices; I4 Values Alignment strong.

math_check.validator_check: Note: math gives 86.3 not 95.0 — research recommends re-examination of published 97.5 baseline; raw means must be 4.9 to yield 97.5 — published baseline appears overstated

calculation_note: Published composite 97.5 implies dimension mean of 4.9 — but published per-dimension scores are uniformly 4.5 which yields composite 87.5. The published composite of 97.5 contains a calculation error or applies an undocumented uplift. This rotation cycle FIRST-BASELINE assessment recommends the score be confirmed at composite ~87.5 (Exemplary), not 97.5. Recommend index correction in a future maintenance cycle.
negativeSignals: Limited publicly disclosed independent outcome evaluations; limited employee culture data; small-company scaling risks (BND).
positiveSignals: Children-as-young-as-5 access; 5x cost reduction; community-input design; 800+ US clinical partner locations; 2025 Hero Pro/RGD product expansion; mission-aligned brand consistency.
sector_context: Open Bionics is the highest-ranked entity in the robotics-labs index. Healthcare/Accessibility category remains a distinct cluster from defense robotics (Boston Dynamics, etc.) and clearly leads in mission-alignment metrics. The composite-vs-dimension calculation discrepancy is a methodology hygiene flag for the index.
open_actions_resolved: Pediatric-5 access captured as EQU/EMP positive evidence
open_actions_carried: Recommend index maintenance review of Open Bionics published composite (97.5 vs calculated 87.5)
Re-queue Open Bionics for second rotation cycle with focus on independent outcome evaluation evidence and employee culture
Monitor for any post-2025 supply-chain or clinical-outcome disclosures
